citations.narrative:
The following housekeeping issues were noted at the animal units:
There is unkept and overgrown grass and weeds present around the perimeter of the sheep and goat unit. Among the
weeds, foxtails were noted both within and outside the pasture where cattle are grazing. Predators can easily hide in tall
grass, and rodents and other pests may nest and take residence in these areas, increasing the potential of injury and
disease hazards to animals. Foxtails may cause discomfort and injury to animals as they can migrate into areas such as
haircoats, ears, and eyes.
There are two large storage spaces where animal food, tools, and maintenance supplies are not maintained as required.
For example, there is a heavy amount of dust and debris on stored items and on the floors, cobwebs are present on the
walls and corners, grain is haphazardly stacked, there is a worktable where irrigation and other supplies are piled up, and
it is not clear what items are no longer being used and can be discarded. The current condition of these spaces may
increase disease hazards to animal areas.
Premises (buildings and grounds) shall be kept clean and in good repair in order to protect the animals from injury and to
facilitate the prescribed husbandry practices set forth in this subpart. Accumulations of trash shall be placed in designated
areas and cleared as necessary to protect the health of the animals.
To be corrected by April 30, 2026
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
n

narrative:
3.131(c) 
   
Sanitation. 
The following housekeeping issues were noted at the animal units: 
There is unkept and overgrown grass and weeds present around the perimeter of the sheep and goat unit. Among the 
weeds, foxtails were noted both within and outside the pasture where cattle are grazing. Predators can easily hide in tall 
grass, and rodents and other pests may nest and take residence in these areas, increasing the potential of injury and 
disease hazards to animals. Foxtails may cause discomfort and injury to animals as they can migrate into areas such as 
haircoats, ears, and eyes. 
 
There are two large storage spaces where animal food, tools, and maintenance supplies are not maintained as required. 
For example, there is a heavy amount of dust and debris on stored items and on the floors, cobwebs are present on the 
walls and corners, grain is haphazardly stacked, there is a worktable where irrigation and other supplies are piled up, and 
it is not clear what items are no longer being used and can be discarded. The current condition of these spaces may 
increase disease hazards to animal areas.  
 
Premises (buildings and grounds) shall be kept clean and in good repair in order to protect the animals from injury and to 
facilitate the prescribed husbandry practices set forth in this subpart. Accumulations of trash shall be placed in designated 
areas and cleared as necessary to protect the health of the animals. 
To be corrected by April 30, 2026 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
